mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in labor market conditions signals an impending economic contraction, prompting investors to price in lower corporate profit growth and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the predictive Rank IC exceeds 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous inflationary pressure and collapsing consumer credit demand indicate a stagflationary environment that erodes real corporate margins and suppresses equity valuations, yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample instances, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained deceleration in industrial production growth reflects weakening aggregate supply momentum, which historically precedes equity drawdowns as corporate capacity utilization falls and profit margins compress over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in over 65% of triggered episodes, or the rolling Rank IC never exceeds 0.04 across any 5-year test period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts serve as a leading indicator of broader consumer confidence and durable goods spending, directly supporting corporate revenue expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances, or the predictive Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds a restrictive threshold, the cost of capital rises sufficiently to suppress equity risk premiums and trigger portfolio rebalancing away from equities, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not significantly lower than the baseline period (t-test p > 0.05), or the signal generates positive returns in more than 50% of high-rate episodes.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of slowing nonfarm payroll growth and a rising unemployment rate confirms a labor market inflection point, signaling imminent earnings downgrades and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to predict negative returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample instances, or the joint signal's Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth paired with contracting industrial output indicates a demand-driven supply bottleneck that forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, suppressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces positive 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the predictive Rank IC never exceeds 0.04 across any 2-year rolling window.
